tweets.text:
Bearish investor positioning in gold options is extremely crowded:

The 6-month put-call skew on the largest US gold-backed ETF, $GLD, is up to 1.03, near the highest since 2017.

The put-call skew measures the relative cost of put options versus call options, rising when investors are paying more to protect against downside than to bet on further upside.

This metric has risen +0.13 points over the last few weeks, posting the largest increase since the 2022 bear market.

By comparison, the 10-year average of the 6-month put-call skew is 0.90.

Meanwhile, the 3-month put skew on $GLD is up to 1.19, the highest on record.

This measures demand for downside protection, and it rises when investors are willing to pay more to hedge against losses.

The short gold trade has become very crowded.

tweets.text:
Where is the US' gold stockpile?

Fort Knox currently holds ~147.3 million troy ounces of gold, the most of any US location, representing ~60% of the total US gold reserves managed by the Treasury.

Fort Knox is the US Bullion Depository in Kentucky and has served as the primary storage site for gold owned outright by the US government since 1937.

By comparison, West Point in New York holds ~54.1 million troy ounces of gold, and Denver in Colorado holds ~43.9 million troy ounces.

Both facilities also serve as active US Mint coin production sites alongside their role as gold depositories.

Meanwhile, the New York Fed vault holds just ~13.4 million troy ounces, or 9% of Fort Knox.

Fort Knox is the backbone of US gold reserves.

Institutional investors are extremely bullish on US stocks:

Last week, hedge funds recorded their largest weekly purchases of US equities since November 2025.

This was primarily led by short covering rather than outright long purchases.

This also marks the 4th consecutive weekly purchase.

As a result, the information technology sector now accounts for ~22% of total US hedge fund equity exposure, near the highest in 5 years.

This percentage has surged +6 points since January, significantly outpacing the increase following the 2022 bear market recovery.

Hedge funds are benefiting from the tech rally.

The fact so many people have been brainwashed to believe the success of Bitcoin is dependent on one man and one company is what's beyond stupid.

I believe Strategy will continue to prioritize paying STRC dividends. They don't really have much of a choice.

The issue is - they're forced to either sell equity (aka MSTR ATM) or sell Bitcoin to fund those payments + debt obligations in the next couple of years.

With the recent Bitcoin purchases + cash raises, they've signaled their preferred route is diluting MSTR shareholders.

And unfortunately there most likely will be a tethered selloff at some point, but that doesn't mean either Strategy or Bitcoin will die.

Strategy will survive, but their cash raising vehicle will be impaired as they deal with rebuilding trust.

Bitcoin will bounce back and print new highs like it always has.

So yes, there easily can be a scenario where MSTR is significantly outperformed by BTC.

tweets.text:
Stablecoin-native neobanks are the model closest to replacing parts of traditional banking.

Plasma One launched around a stablecoin balance that behaves more like an account than a simple crypto card. The product extends that balance into transfers, FX, local payment rails, and everyday spend, which makes the card part of a broader account experience.

Standalone card economics are thin. Interchange is only 1-2% per transaction before rewards, fraud losses, chargebacks, processing costs, and issuing-partner economics reduce what the front end keeps. A card can generate spend, and the business becomes more durable when users hold balances inside the account before they transact.

Plasma One grew 345% from April to May while still in private beta and crossed $8.5M in card spend before launch. The main question is whether users are holding balances inside Plasma One before they spend. That behavior is what makes the account the product.

This matter most in markets where dollar access, cross-border payments, and local spend still break down. Plasma One tests whether a stablecoin balance can become the account users rely on across those flows.

It looks like F3 #Uranium $FUU is valued only based on the JR Zone.

But the Tetra discovery seems to be significant as well.

After reading the latest update, I think the market may be overlooking something even bigger:

The sheer scale of the exploration pipeline across the entire Patterson Lake North project.

$FUU now controls nearly 43,000 hectares in one of the most prospective uranium districts in the world, surrounded by NexGen's Arrow and Paladin's Triple R deposits.

What stood out in this release:

✅ Multiple new drill-ready targets identified across all three PLN properties

✅ Four priority targets around Tetra with geophysical signatures similar to the discovery itself

✅ Historic core sampling returned uranium values and intense alteration, yet several targets remain largely untested

✅ A 7 km-long conductive trend at Minto showing similarities to the conductor hosting the JR Zone

The most important comment in the release came from VP Exploration Sam Hartmann:

Tetra was discovered in an area that many explorers had effectively overlooked.

Instead of following conventional thinking, F3 applied a different geological model and made what became the first uranium discovery in the Clearwater Domain.

Now they're applying that same exploration approach across the broader land package.

That's what makes this exciting.

JR is already a significant discovery.

Tetra continues to grow.

And now the company has multiple additional targets moving into the drill-ready stage.

In the Athabasca Basin, one discovery can create enormous value.

A district with multiple discoveries can create something much larger.

With drilling starting in early July, the next catalyst cycle is just around the corner.

Disclosure: Paid partnership with F3 Uranium. Not financial advice. Please do your own due diligence.

The best part is you can get access onchain.

Trade it 24/7, long or short, with leverage your broker won't give you.

Here's where to do it:

#1 Synthetic perps

This is for leverage, shorting and 24/7 speculation. You're not owning SpaceX shares. You're trading a contract that tracks the price.

• @HyperliquidX is the main venue right now. $SPCX has become one of the biggest HIP-3 markets and even crossed $2B+ 24H volume.

• • @Aster_DEX has stock perps with high leverage. Currently doing around ~$26.4M 24H volume and ~$3.08M OI on SPCX

• @Lighter_xyz is the ZK perp DEX w/ fast execution and growing equity perp support. ~$35.5M 24H vol with ~$11.1M OI and ~0.039% spread on SPCX

• @StandX_Official lets traders use yield-bearing DUSD as margin while trading perps.

• @extendedapp has been pushing pre-IPO & equity perps with a CEX-like trading experience on @Starknet.

• @PhoenixTrade is the @solana perp w/ low fees, fast execution and fully onchain equity / RWA perps.

• @nadoHQ is building a CLOB-style venue for spot + perps, with the • @inkonchain ecosystem.

#2 Tokenized Spot exposure

If you don't want leverage, here are a few options:

• @OndoFinance has $SPCXon w/ 1:1 SPCX Shares Per Token, available on Eth, BNB Chain, and Solana.

• @Backpack has tokenized $SPCX built around 1:1 backed equity exposure, with Solana-native trading.

• @xStocksFi has $SPCXX, a tracker certificate token that follows SpaceX stock price across Solana and EVM chains.

• @PreStocks is more focused on private / pre-IPO exposure.

Now here's what you're actually trading.

$SPCX is sitting at a massive $2.5T FDV with only ~4.2% of shares actually floating right now.

Massive brand, tiny float, insane attention, and unlocks sitting in the background. Retail's probably going to treat this like a memecoin until more supply opens up.

The bigger story isn't just SpaceX. Every hyped IPO after this probably gets traded the same way.

Some people will use perps for leverage and shorting. Others will use tokenized spot for cleaner exposure.

Both come with their own risks, but the demand is clearly there.

We track thousands of data points every day to stay on top of these developments in real time, while institutions like the ECB still take 3–4 weeks to produce their staff projections. Impressively bad.

Inflation is now clearly trending lower, liquidity is clearly trending higher, and growth remains flat but on a somewhat weaker trajectory. We call this regime “QE-like.”

Looking across our pattern-recognition model, these are the trades it currently favors:

Overall, this is a short USD, long metals and industrials type of environment. It is also very bearish on oil, although we obviously do not have many comparable periods for oil. Strong gains are still expected in EWY (Korea) and semiconductors, but leadership is likely to rotate from here.

tweets.text:
There's a confirmed airdrop landing between July and September.

The money's already starting to flow in, but there's still time to farm.

It's Kraken's Ink. Over $397M bridged in over the last 30 days. I went through the whole ecosystem and here's some opportunities I see:

Kraken

Start with @krakenpro. It's the most direct play since you earn Ink points for trading volume, deposits, and staking.

Ink is Kraken's own L2, so my bet is Kraken users get the heaviest weighting when the criteria drop. That's why it's first on my list.

Tydro

@tydrohq is the lazy farm. It's the largest native lending protocol on Ink, and the mechanic's dead simple: more lending and borrowing means more points.

If you want to keep it passive, this is the one to park activity in.

Nado

@nadoHQ is the one that's actually got me interested. It's been generating the most daily revenue on Ink recently, which is probably what's driving the inflow surge in the first place.

But here's what makes it different. On Nado you earn Nado points, not INK points, and the team confirmed those convert to $INK at TGE.

So there's a real chance you're farming two potential airdrops off one position, Ink and Nado.

Season 2 is running now with points going out weekly, and they launched a $100k trading comp yesterday.

xStocks

One more protocol I'd layer in: @xStocksFi doesn't have a direct INK farming mechanism, but it integrates with Nado, so you can use tokenized equities as collateral on Nado and stack xStocks points on top of everything else.

2 birds one stone without much effort.

Timing

@buyerofponzi advises both Ink and Nado, and he confirmed the July to September window in an interview with @nothreadguy.

The best airdrops close before most people hear about them, and this is shaping up to be one of those.

Get positioned now.

I’d say it’s easy enough for a terminally online crypto user to buy their own basket, but many people don’t have the time or desire to do that and would gladly outsource it to someone else — I don’t get why it has to be an anon tho, why can’t trusted/known personalities (or even companies) launch their own indexes?

There are still issues with the design and i also don’t see it taking off as it stands, but with one tweaks/improvements I think there’s a lot of merit to the concept

Maybe it’s prism/spectrum that figures it out, maybe it’s some other project in the future, but onchain index funds still seem like a great great great concept to me

DECISION IMMINENT: US energy regulator FERC is poised to rule by end of June on how data centers connect to the grid. Former FERC Chairman Neil Chatterjee calls it "the most significant FERC action in decades."

Two tracks to watch:
1) PJM co-location rules: clarifying whether hyperscalers can buy power directly from on-site generators without triggering tariff violations
2) A broader DOE-directed federal rulemaking that would standardize large load interconnection nationwide and may require hyperscalers to foot the full bill for grid upgrades

Existing grid interconnects become more valuable if hyperscalers must self-fund upgrades, but even more importantly, regulatory certainty alone is likely enough to kick off a new investment cycle in PJM, where new capacity has badly lagged demand. For context, PJM fell 6.6 GW short in its December 2025 capacity auction, with capacity prices hitting a record $333/MW-day.

Could be good catalyst for PJM-exposed names: $TLN $CEG $WULF $KEEL $MARA

The hardest thing in business is not seeing the future. It is surviving long enough to build it.

My fireside chat with @Julian_Liniger at @BTCPrague on focus, endurance, corporate transformation, and how entrepreneurs can use Bitcoin, AI, and digital finance to create the next generation of products.

Full interview below.

00:00 - Bitcoin as the dominant global Digital Capital network: 17 years, hundreds of billions invested, and a potential $100T opportunity
00:51 - Bitcoin near the 200-week moving average: why $BTC is more compelling after a 50% drawdown
01:52 - Strategy’s scale and the media narrative: from ~$600M enterprise value to as high as ~$120B
10:29 - Bitcoin fundamentals: economic empowerment, sovereign property rights, and the dominant digital monetary network
12:16 - Why there is no second best: Bitcoin as Digital Capital, Digital Money, and a potential $100T network
16:09 - Entrepreneur advice: build a simple product using new technology to solve a real problem
20:30 - Focus, endurance, and the danger of dilutive distractions
32:25 - What I would build today: AI plus Digital Assets, especially Digital Money and Digital Yield
33:27 - Digital Credit: taking a 40 vol asset, stripping it to ~4 vol, and creating new yield products
34:57 - Digital Money: 6–8% yield in major currencies with no volatility
38:05 - $STRC, $SATA, and the next layer of bitcoin-backed financial products
48:52 - Q&A: why Strategy sold 32 BTC and why bitcoin-backed capital must support credit and equity
59:29 - Q&A: Strategy as a shock absorber: selling 32 BTC while buying net ~250,000 BTC during the bear market
01:02:39 - Why public companies protect Bitcoin through accounting, tax, legal, political, and economic advocacy
01:07:58 - Strategy as the extension of the Bitcoin network into the free market system

The discrepancy is getting even larger, with STRC ticking sub $92 yesterday while SATA is trading at par.

SATA is 'only' paying 1.5% more per year, which shouldn't be enough to justify such a difference, I'm personally convinced that the market has no clue how to price these prefs correctly.

The nature of the holder base as well as the market cap of the pref does matter a lot. STRC increased from like $3bn mcap to $10bn mcap in 3 months, which implies that there is a LOT of rotations (from old/panicking to new holders) needed during each "depeg" to get back to par.

Strategy will increase their dividend rate to 12% in July, then probably even more in August. STRC will end up trading back to par, but it can take time. If BTC dumps further down, it will take EVEN more time.

But as long as STRC is trading below par, there are no inflows into BTC from STRC, so at least we can see BTC trade 'organically' without people trying to frontrun the STRC inflows.

tweets.text:
Something I’m wondering as I’m beginning to learn piano is how likely is it in the next 30 years that we could inject « skills » like « top piano players » via brain-computer interfaces similar to Neuralink? 

Like an artificial neuron (AI) enhancing my not artificial neurons? 

Some options:

- The device could detect I’m about to hit the wrong note and correct my motor intent before I even make the mistake.

- Not injecting skill but compressing the time it takes to learn. I could practice 10 minutes instead of 10 years. 

- The device modifies my synapses connections for me to become a top piano player right away 

There is a sort of nihilism associated with AGI where I’m always thinking: is that worth it or the model will help me do it in minutes instead of years soon? 🫠
